name: Mark Ryden
description: Mark Ryden is widely credited with founding Pop Surrealism — his hyper-detailed oil paintings blend Victorian portraiture, religious iconography, meat, and wide-eyed children into images of deeply unsettling beauty. Ryden coined the term 'Pop Surrealism' and his 1998 Meltdown show at Koplin Gallery became a landmark moment for the Lowbrow movement. His work bridges fine art and mass culture with meticulous Old Master technique.
